reference_review:
SUMMARY:
Thanks very much for the PR, @eddumelendez. You're quite prolific! I've left a few comments for your consideration when you have time.

POINTS:
- [spring-boot-project/spring-boot-autoconfigure/src/main/java/org/springframework/boot/autoconfigure/jms/activemq/ActiveMQConnectionFactoryFactory.java:84] In addition to the broker URL, I think that `user` and `password` should come from the connection details as well, otherwise it won't be possible to use connected to an ActiveMQ service that requires authentication.
- [spring-boot-project/spring-boot-autoconfigure/src/main/java/org/springframework/boot/autoconfigure/jms/activemq/PropertiesActiveMQConnectionDetails.java:25] I think this could be package-private, and perhaps an inner-class of the `@Configuration` where it's defined.
- [spring-boot-project/spring-boot-autoconfigure/src/main/java/org/springframework/boot/autoconfigure/jms/activemq/ConnectionDetailsConfiguration.java:26] Do we need a separate class here? Instead, could the bean be defined directly in the auto-configuration class that imports this class?
- [spring-boot-project/spring-boot-autoconfigure/src/main/java/org/springframework/boot/autoconfigure/jms/activemq/ConnectionDetailsConfiguration.java:30] Tiny nit, I'd name this method `activeMqConnectionDetails`.

SUMMARY:
@copilot make sure running the command `mvn clean install -T1C -DskipTests` succeeds to make sure we have no compile errors.

POINTS:
- [p2-maven-plugin/src/main/java/org/eclipse/tycho/p2maven/helper/ProxyHelper.java:1] @copilot this file seems to be completely reformated would it be possible to keep the original formatting and only apply the suggested changes so it is easier to review and get a smaller diff?
- [p2-maven-plugin/src/main/java/org/eclipse/tycho/p2maven/repository/DefaultMavenRepositorySettings.java:49] It seems here also `@Named` annotation is missing maybe on other places as well.
- [p2-maven-plugin/src/main/java/org/eclipse/tycho/p2maven/repository/DefaultSignedContentFactory.java:33] Same as above `@Named` is missing!
- [p2-maven-plugin/src/main/java/org/eclipse/tycho/p2maven/repository/EclipsePluginArtifactHandler.java:20] Here and in a few other files there is only an single character `A` what is invalid and likely should refer to the hint value previously used in the `@Component`.

This currently fails the build if one runs `mvn clean install -T1C -DskipTests` in the root of the repository.
- [p2-maven-plugin/src/main/java/org/eclipse/tycho/p2maven/repository/P2ArtifactRepositoryLayout.java:24] Another example where the hint was not properly converted

reference_review:
SUMMARY:
This is on the right track. See comments inline.

POINTS:
- [org.eclipse.wildwebdeveloper.xml/plugin.xml:150] elements should use lowercase (`classpathExtensionProvider`)
- [org.eclipse.wildwebdeveloper.xml/plugin.xml:151] lower case: `provider`
- [org.eclipse.wildwebdeveloper.xml/schema/xmllsClasspathExtensionProvider.exsd:70] 0.9.0 IIRC.
- [org.eclipse.wildwebdeveloper.xml/src/org/eclipse/wildwebdeveloper/xml/BundleResolver.java:37] Do you need to start the bundle to get its resources? I'm don't think so.
- [org.eclipse.wildwebdeveloper.xml/src/org/eclipse/wildwebdeveloper/xml/MavenArtifactClassPathProvider.java:17] This should be in the m2e patch that's currently on Gerrit.
- [org.eclipse.wildwebdeveloper.xml/src/org/eclipse/wildwebdeveloper/xml/MavenArtifactClassPathProvider.java:13] Missing legal header.
- [org.eclipse.wildwebdeveloper.xml/src/org/eclipse/wildwebdeveloper/xml/XMLLSClassPathExtensionRegistry.java:28] Actually, I'm not sure you really need to keep a copy of the model here. Maybe it's easier and more powerful and cheap enough to query the extension registry everytime it's needed. WDYT?
- [org.eclipse.wildwebdeveloper.xml/src/org/eclipse/wildwebdeveloper/xml/XMLLSClassPathExtensionRegistry.java:13] legal header
- [org.eclipse.wildwebdeveloper.xml/src/org/eclipse/wildwebdeveloper/xml/XMLLSClasspathExtensionProvider.java:13] Legal header.
- [org.eclipse.wildwebdeveloper.xml/src/org/eclipse/wildwebdeveloper/xml/XMLLSClasspathExtensionProvider.java:22] add a `@since 0.9.0` annotation here.

POINTS:
- [core/deployment/src/main/java/io/quarkiverse/mcp/server/deployment/McpServerProcessor.java:53] If the OTel dependency is optional, we cannot import the class directly. This also applies to `io.quarkus.opentelemetry.runtime.config.runtime.OTelRuntimeConfig`.
- [core/runtime/src/main/java/io/quarkiverse/mcp/server/runtime/tracing/McpRequestInfo.java:15] This could be derived from the MCP transport, right? If so, then `mcpRequest.connection().initialRequest().transport()` could be used.
- [core/runtime/src/main/java/io/quarkiverse/mcp/server/runtime/McpRequestImpl.java:26] I think that this should be at least `volatile`.
- [core/runtime/src/main/java/io/quarkiverse/mcp/server/runtime/McpRequestImpl.java:111] Why do we actually store a copy? The object itself is immutable (except for the `responseInfo`) so a copy does not make a lot of sense to me.

Do we actually have to store something in the duplicated context? Couldn't we just store this information in the `McpRequest`?

REPO: sevntu-checkstyle/sevntu.checkstyle PR #722: Issue #464: Check for trailing commas on enums Changed files (11): eclipsecs-sevntu-plugin/src/com/github/sevntu/checkstyle/checks/coding/checkstyle-metadata.properties, eclipsecs-sevntu-plugin/src/com/github/sevntu/checkstyle/checks/coding/checkstyle-metadata.xml, sevntu-checks/sevntu-checks.xml, sevntu-checks/src/main/java/com/github/sevntu/checkstyle/checks/coding/EnumTrailingCommaCheck.java, sevntu-checks/src/main/java/com/github/sevntu/checkstyle/checks/coding/NumericLiteralNeedsUnderscoreCheck.java, sevntu-checks/src/main/resources/com/github/sevntu/checkstyle/checks/coding/messages.properties, sevntu-checks/src/test/java/com/github/sevntu/checkstyle/checks/coding/EnumTrailingCommaCheckTest.java, sevntu-checks/src/test/java/com/github/sevntu/checkstyle/internal/CheckstyleRegressionTest.java, sevntu-checks/src/test/java/com/github/sevntu/checkstyle/internal/CommitValidationTest.java, sevntu-checks/src/test/resources/com/github/sevntu/checkstyle/checks/coding/InputEnumTrailingCommaCheck.java, sevntu-checkstyle-sonar-plugin/src/main/resources/com/github/sevntu/checkstyle/sonar/checkstyle-extensions.xml Diff (+317/-4): PR DESCRIPTION: Addresses #464, supersedes #465, #474, and #709 This Check assures that the last enum constant definition in a multi-line enum ends in a comma: ``` enum E1 { ONE, TWO, THREE, } ``` Usage examples are in the Javadoc of `EnumTrailingCommaCheck`. This check follows the concept of [ArrayTrailingComma](http://checkstyle.sourceforge.net/config_coding.html#ArrayTrailingComma), hence the similar name. It also checks that the "trailing" comma after the last enum constant definition is not followed by a semicolon, because this would miss the point of the check: clean diff and easy manipulation. DIFF: --- eclipsecs-sevntu-plugin/src/com/github/sevntu/checkstyle/checks/coding/checkstyle-metadata.properties (+3/-0) --- @@ -183,6 +183,9 @@ EitherLogOrThrowCheck.loggingMethodNames = Logging method names separated with c WhitespaceBeforeArrayInitializerCheck.name = Whitespace Before Array Initializer WhitespaceBeforeArrayInitializerCheck.desc = This checks enforces whitespace before array initializer. +EnumTrailingCommaCheck.name = Enum Trailing Comma +EnumTrailingCommaCheck.desc = This checks enforces a trailing comma at the end of a line in an enum constant definition. + MoveVariableInsideIfCheck.name = Move Variable Inside If Check MoveVariableInsideIfCheck.desc = Checks if a variable is only used inside if statements and asks for it's declaration to be moved there too. --- eclipsecs-sevntu-plugin/src/com/github/sevntu/checkstyle/checks/coding/checkstyle-metadata.xml (+7/-0) --- @@ -556,5 +556,12 @@ + + + %EnumTrailingCommaCheck.desc + + + + --- sevntu-checks/sevntu-checks.xml (+1/-0) --- @@ -157,6 +157,7 @@ + --- sevntu-checks/src/main/java/com/github/sevntu/checkstyle/checks/coding/EnumTrailingCommaCheck.java (+142/-0) --- @@ -0,0 +1,142 @@ +//////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////// +// checkstyle: Checks Java source code for adherence to a set of rules. +// Copyright (C) 2001-2018 the original author or authors. +// +// This library is free software; you can redistribute it and/or +// modify it under the terms of the GNU Lesser General Public +// License as published by the Free Software Foundation; either +// version 2.1 of the License, or (at your option) any later version. +// +// This library is distributed in the hope that it will be useful, +// but WITHOUT ANY WARRANTY; without even the implied warranty of +// MERCHANTABILITY or FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE. See the GNU +// Lesser General Public License for more details. +// +// You should have received a copy of the GNU Lesser General Public +// License along with this library; if not, write to the Free Software +// Foundation, Inc., 59 Temple Place, Suite 330, Boston, MA 02111-1307 USA +//////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////// + +package com.github.sevntu.checkstyle.checks.coding; + +import com.puppycrawl.tools.checkstyle.api.AbstractCheck; +import com.puppycrawl.tools.checkstyle.api.DetailAST; +import com.puppycrawl.tools.checkstyle.api.TokenTypes; + +/** + * Checks if enum constant contains an optional trailing comma. + * + *

Rationale: Putting this comma in makes is easier to change the order of the elements or add + * new elements at the end of the list. This is similar to + * ArrayTrailingComma. + * + *

The following is a normal enum type declaration: + * 

+ * enum Type {
+ *     ALPHA,
+ *     BETA,
+ *     GAMMA
+ * }
+ *
+ * + *

However, if you want to append something to the list, you would need to change the line + * containing the last enum constant: + * + * 

+ * enum Type {
+ *     ALPHA,
+ *     BETA,
+ *     GAMMA,   // changed due to the ','
+ *     DELTA    // new line
+ * }
+ *
+ * + *

This check makes sure that also the last enum constant has a trailing comma, which is + * valid according to the Java Spec (see Enum Constants) + * + * 

+ * enum Type {
+ *     ALPHA,
+ *     BETA,
+ *     GAMMA,
+ *     DELTA, // removing this comma will result in a violation with the check activated
+ * }
+ *
+ * + *

However, you could also add a semicolon behind that comma on the same line, which would raise + * a violation + * + * 

+ * enum Type {
+ *     ALPHA,
+ *     BETA,
+ *     GAMMA,
+ *     DELTA,; // violation
+ * }
+ *
+ * In this case the semicolon should be removed. However, if there is more in the enum body, the + * semicolon should be placed on a line by itself. + * + *

An example of how to configure the check is: + * 

+ * <module name="EnumTrailingComma"/>
+ *
+ * + *

Plea

SUMMARY:
when I review code I read Check goals and code .... that is why I see bunch of mismatches in goals and code/implementation. Lets clarify goals of Check.

I need more details on expected behavior:

POINTS:
- [eclipsecs-sevntu-plugin/src/com/github/sevntu/checkstyle/checks/coding/checkstyle-metadata.properties:187] https://docs.oracle.com/javase/specs/jls/se8/html/jls-8.html#jls-8.9.1

please remove "line" , this Check looks like care only about "coma after Enum Constant List" only.
If this is not true, than logic need to be changed.
- [sevntu-checks/src/main/java/com/github/sevntu/checkstyle/checks/coding/EnumTrailingCommaCheck.java:27] let make it absolutely clear that Check do no not validate all trailing commas, it demand presence  of only one comma that is after enum constant list. Please make a link to jdk specification 
https://docs.oracle.com/javase/specs/jls/se8/html/jls-8.html#jls-8.9.1
- [sevntu-checks/src/main/java/com/github/sevntu/checkstyle/checks/coding/EnumTrailingCommaCheck.java:29] there should be mentioned that Rationale is applicable only for cases when enum is formatted to have enum constant separate on line.
if Enum is single line - there is no benefit of this check. 


Should this Check skip single line enums ? in other case  what is a benefit of this Check on code like:
`enum COLOR { RED, BLACK }` ?
It might be ok to demand come here too, but Rationale need to be updated. I am not sure how to update it. Please suggest something, as you are author of this idea, you might know more use cases.
- [sevntu-checks/src/main/java/com/github/sevntu/checkstyle/checks/coding/EnumTrailingCommaCheck.java:94] it depends probably on your replies above, but looks like, name of Check should be  EnumConstantListTrailingCommaCheck , no need change right now, lets wait for clarification on Check behavior and rationale

POINTS:
- [server/src/main/java/org/jboss/as/server/ServerEnvironment.java:508] Could you translate the alias here outside of the ConfigurationFile?
- [host-controller/src/main/java/org/jboss/as/host/controller/HostControllerEnvironment.java:479] This is not a domain mode feature, so if we translate the alias outside of the ConfigurationFile, we can leave this part as it was without any modification.
- [host-controller/src/main/java/org/jboss/as/host/controller/HostControllerConfigurationPersister.java:235] This is not a domain mode feature, so if we translate the alias outside of the ConfigurationFile, we can leave this part as it was without any modification.
- [controller/src/main/java/org/jboss/as/controller/persistence/ConfigurationFile.java:365] After looking at the changes implemented, I think we should extract the `translateFileAlias` method outside of the ConfigurationFile to avoid any side effects, for example, at this moment the `bootFileName` field class will get the alias name, instead of the translated file name.

If we extract the `translateFileAlias` method and move it to ServerEnvironment.java, we can leave any domain mode-related classes untouched and have this only applied to standalone files. That would also avoid the need to pass Stability to the `ConfigurationFile` constructors. The Stabilitily.enables(...) invocation could be also invoked in this `translateFileAlias`, which can be a static method so it can be easily tested in a Unit Test.

SUMMARY:
The changes look like a good refactoring to better encapsulate the management of handles for zooms and in general are fine fore me. I still have some proposals for improvements.

POINTS:
- [bundles/org.eclipse.swt/Eclipse SWT/win32/org/eclipse/swt/graphics/Image.java:142] Since this is to a certain degree a wrapper for a data structure map, wouldn't it maybe be sufficient to use according short method names rather than always repeating "handle" and "imageHandle"? I.e.:
- getImageHandle(zoom) -> get(zoom)
- getHandleOrCreate(zoom, creator) -> getOrCreate(zoom, creator)
- hasImageHandle(handle) -> contains(handle)
- hasZoom(zoom) -> contains(zoom) / containsHandleFor(zoom)
- hasNoHandle() -> isEmpty()
- [bundles/org.eclipse.swt/Eclipse SWT/win32/org/eclipse/swt/graphics/Image.java:2655] Since init(data, zoom) does not register the handle at the map anymore, is it correct that we don't do it at this consumer?
- [bundles/org.eclipse.swt/Eclipse SWT/win32/org/eclipse/swt/graphics/Image.java:2512] There is already `imageHandleManager.hasImageHandle()` for this. But I think it would be more clear in general if we explicitly store locally that the image needs to be destroyed. E.g., we could simply have a boolean `destroyNativeImage` that is set to true when we use `initNative` and only destroy the handle then.
- [bundles/org.eclipse.swt/Eclipse SWT/win32/org/eclipse/swt/graphics/Image.java:212] The `hasImageHandle` method is only used here and rather indicates a missing local state handling, I would propose to remove that method and manage the state locally instead.
The method is used to find out, if this `HandleAtSize` has created the handle on its own or whether it was retrieved from the `ImageHandleManager`. So the `HandleAtSize` could just store that it created the handle on its own (inside `refresh()`) and then just use that information here to determine if the handleContainer needs to be destroyed or not instead of indirectly retrieving that information again by querying the ImageHandleManager for whether it contains that handle.
- [bundles/org.eclipse.swt/Eclipse SWT/win32/org/eclipse/swt/graphics/GC.java:4471] Why are all these changes to `GC` necessary?

- [src/main/java/io/vertx/core/MultiMap.java:70] the return type and the `defaultValue` parameters should be annotated with `@Nullable`
- [src/main/java/io/vertx/core/MultiMap.java:71] no need for `@Nullable` annotating the statement

REPO: eclipse-tycho/tycho
PR #5627: Add JDT classpath attribute support for multi-release JAR compilation
Changed files (14): demo/multi-release-jar-classpath/.classpath, demo/multi-release-jar-classpath/META-INF/MANIFEST.MF, demo/multi-release-jar-classpath/README.md, demo/multi-release-jar-classpath/build.properties, demo/multi-release-jar-classpath/pom.xml, demo/multi-release-jar-classpath/src/tycho/mr/example/HttpClient.java, demo/multi-release-jar-classpath/src/tycho/mr/example/Main.java, demo/multi-release-jar-classpath/src11/tycho/mr/example/HttpClient.java, demo/multi-release-jar-classpath/src9/tycho/mr/example/HttpClient.java, src/site/markdown/BuildingMultiReleaseBundles.md, src/site/site.xml, tycho-compiler-plugin/src/main/java/org/eclipse/tycho/compiler/AbstractOsgiCompilerMojo.java, tycho-its/src/test/java/org/eclipse/tycho/test/DemoTest.java, tycho-metadata-model/src/main/java/org/eclipse/tycho/model/classpath/SourceFolderClasspathEntry.java
Diff (+468/-16):
PR DESCRIPTION:
Implements support for Eclipse JDT's `release` classpath attribute (introduced in Eclipse 4.38) to enable multi-release JAR compilation with flexible source folder naming and without requiring supplemental manifests.

## Changes

### Core Implementation

- **`SourceFolderClasspathEntry`**: Added `getMultiReleaseVersion()` method returning `OptionalInt` to extract Java version from `release` classpath attribute
- **`AbstractOsgiCompilerMojo`**: Enhanced `doCompile()` to check for `Multi-Release: true` manifest header first, then detect classpath entries with `release` attributes, compile them with appropriate `--release` flags, and output to `META-INF/versions/N/` directories. Falls back to directory-based approach for backward compatibility.
- **Manifest Requirement**: The `Multi-Release: true` header must be explicitly set in the manifest as a prerequisite to enable the feature

### Example

`.classpath` file marking source folders for specific Java versions:
```xml
<classpath>
    <classpathentry kind="src" path="src"/>
    <classpathentry kind="src" path="src9">
        <attributes>
            <attribute name="release" value="9"/>
        </attributes>
    </classpathentry>
    <classpathentry kind="src" path="src11">
        <attributes>
            <attribute name="release" value="11"/>
        </attributes>
    </classpathentry>
</classpath>
```

Note: Source folders can be named flexibly (e.g., `src_java9`, `java11-src`). The version is determined by the `release` att

@@ -0,0 +1,17 @@
+<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
+<classpath>
+	<classpathentry kind="src" path="src"/>
+	<classpathentry kind="src" path="src9">
+		<attributes>
+			<attribute name="release" value="9"/>
+		</attributes>
+	</classpathentry>
+	<classpathentry kind="src" path="src11">
+		<attributes>
+			<attribute name="release" value="11"/>
+		</attributes>
+	</classpathentry>
+	<classpathentry kind="con" path="org.eclipse.jdt.launching.JRE_CONTAINER/org.eclipse.jdt.internal.debug.ui.launcher.StandardVMType/JavaSE-1.8"/>
+	<classpathentry kind="con" path="org.eclipse.pde.core.requiredPlugins"/>
+	<classpathentry kind="output" path="bin"/>
+</classpath>
--- demo/multi-release-jar-classpath/META-INF/MANIFEST.MF (+9/-0) ---
@@ -0,0 +1,9 @@
+Manifest-Version: 1.0
+Bundle-ManifestVersion: 2
+Bundle-Name: MR-JAR Classpath TestCase
+Bundle-SymbolicName: mr.classpath
+Bundle-Version: 1.0.0.qualifier
+Export-Package: tycho.mr.example;version="0.0.1"
+Import-Package: org.apache.commons.io
+Multi-Release: true
+Require-Capability: osgi.ee;filter:="(&(osgi.ee=JavaSE)(version=1.8))"
--- demo/multi-release-jar-classpath/README.md (+40/-0) ---
@@ -0,0 +1,40 @@
+# Building Multi-Release-Jar with Classpath Attributes
+
+This sample shows how to build a [Multi-Release-Jar](https://openjdk.org/jeps/238) with Tycho using the JDT classpath attribute approach.
+
+This approach requires the `Multi-Release: true` manifest header but simplifies the build by using Eclipse JDT's `release` classpath attribute to mark source folders for specific Java releases, without requiring special directory structures or supplemental manifests.
+
+## Structure
+
+- `src` - contains the main sources (Java 8)
+- `src9` - contains the source for release 9 (marked with `release="9"` in `.classpath`)
+- `src11` - contains the source for release 11 (marked with `release="11"` in `.classpath`)
+- `META-INF/MANIFEST.MF` - the manifest with `Multi-Release: true` header
+
+Note: Source folders can be named anything (e.g., `src_java9`, `java9-src`), not just `src9` or `src11`.
+
+## Classpath Configuration
+
+The `.classpath` file contains entries like:
+
+```xml
+<classpathentry kind="src" path="src9">
+    <attributes>
+        <attribute name="release" value="9"/>
+    </attributes>
+</classpathentry>
+```
+
+This tells Tycho to compile the sources in `src9` for Java 9 and place them in `META-INF/versions/9/` in the resulting JAR.
+
+## Comparison with Manifest-First Approach
+
+This approach is more flexible than the manifest-first approach because:
+- Source folders can be named flexibly (derived from `.classpath`, not fixed naming convention)
+- No supplemental manifests required in `META-INF/versions/N/OSGI-INF/`
+- Follows Eclipse JDT conventions more closely
+- Easier integration with Eclipse IDE
+
+Both approaches require the `Multi-Release: true` manifest header.
+
+See the `multi-release-jar` demo for the traditional manifest-first approach with fixed directory naming.
--- demo/multi-release-jar-classpath/build.properties (+4/-0) ---
@@ -0,0 +1,4 @@
+source.. = src/
+output.. = bin/
+bin.includes = META-INF/,\
+               .
--- demo/multi-release-jar-classpath/pom.xml (+31/-0) ---
@@ -0,0 +1,31 @@
+<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
+<project xmlns="http://maven.apache.org/POM/4.0.0" xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance"
+	xsi:schemaLocation="http://maven.apache.org/POM/4.0.0 http://maven.apache.org/xsd/maven-4.0.0.xsd">
+	<modelVersion>4.0.0</modelVersion>
+	<groupId>tycho.its.compiler.mr.classpath</groupId>
+	<artifactId>mr.classpath</artifactId>
+	<version>1.0.0-SNAPSHOT</version>
+	<packaging>eclipse-plugin</packaging>
+	<properties>
+		<!-- This values are overridden by the test execution but are usefull if running as a standalone project (e.g. debugging) -->
+		<tycho-version>4.0.0-SNAPSHOT</tycho-version>
+		<target-platform>https://download.eclipse.org/releases/2022-12/</target-platform>
+	</properties>
+	<repositories>
+		<repository>
+			<id>platform</id>
+			<url>${target-platform}</url>
+			<layout>p2</layout>
+		</repository>
+	</repositories>
+	<build>
+		<plugins>
+			<plugin>
+				<groupId>org.eclipse.tycho</groupId>
+				<artifactId>tycho-maven-plugin</artifactId>
+				<version>${tycho-version}</version>
+				<extensions>true</extensions>
+			</plugin>
+		</plugins>
+	</build>
+</project>
--- demo/multi-release-jar-classpath/src/tycho/m
reference_review:
SUMMARY:
@copilot please look at the review comments some things have to be adjusted here.

POINTS:
- [demo/multi-release-jar-classpath/README.md:5] The attribute is not named `multirelease` it is named `release` this has to be fixed all over the place in this PR.
- [demo/multi-release-jar-classpath/README.md:31] Also the `Multi-Release: true` header *must* be set this should be our primary trigger, but we do not want to 

1. require any Supplemental Manifests
2. require the source folders to be named in a specific way (as we no longer derive the release from the path but from the attribute)
- [src/site/markdown/BuildingMultiReleaseBundles.md:172] Classpath: its should even be possible to name the folder like `src_java9` as we always can derive the name+release from the classpath

Manifest-First: not only requires the META-INF/version structure but also the fixed source folder names!
- [tycho-compiler-plugin/src/main/java/org/eclipse/tycho/compiler/AbstractOsgiCompilerMojo.java:390] According to the spec the manifest header MUST be set so it won't work otherwise (e.g. in IDEs), also we do not want take extra work for projects that have not explicitly enabled it (like the same goal in the MR-JAR spec!)

As mentioned above, this check should be remain! and then we should only check for the classpath attributes first inside the if-block.
- [tycho-metadata-model/src/main/java/org/eclipse/tycho/model/classpath/SourceFolderClasspathEntry.java:35] Use OptionalInt here instead of Integer.
- [tycho-packaging-plugin/src/main/java/org/eclipse/tycho/packaging/PackagePluginMojo.java:392] We should not set this automatically, it is a prerequisite to enable the feature at all
